title: Space boom strains supply chain, industry report warns
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to keep up with demand as it was built for smaller volumes and slower production cycles. A new report warns of potential disruptions.

title: ISPTech Raises €5.5M Seed Round to Redefine How Spacecraft Manoeuvre in Orbit
summary: ISPTech, a German space technology company, raises €5.5 million in seed funding for agile in-orbit maneuvering propulsion systems

title: Canadian military invests in sovereign launch
summary: Canadian military invests in launch facilities and companies for sovereign space access capability. Major announcement made March 16.

title: An astronaut’s tiny stand-in: tissue chips in space health
summary: Tiny replicas of organs may travel to space before humans, testing how bodies respond to cosmic radiation and weightlessness. Organ-on-a-chip technology uses cells from astronauts' own bodies.

title: Where are all the aliens? Maybe they just don't want to talk to us
summary: A possible explanation for the lack of alien contact is that a civilization capable of interstellar travel may have moved beyond certain destructive tendencies.

title: Ryugu asteroid sample contains all five key components of DNA, scientists find
summary: A sample collected from Ryugu asteroid contains all five key components of DNA, including adenine, guanine, cytosine, thymine, and uracil.

title: Dim Delights in Cancer
summary: Cancer contains a beautiful star cluster, the Beehive Cluster, and one of the most studied exoplanets, 55 Cancri e.

title: Solving Asteroid Bennu’s Mysteries
summary: NASA solved a mystery about asteroid Bennu's surface after releasing X-ray computed tomography scans, showing common crack networks observed in Bennu samples.

title: Asteroid Bennu’s Rugged Surface Baffled NASA, We Finally Know Why
summary: NASA's OSIRIS-REx mission found asteroid Bennu to be a jagged, rugged world with few smooth patches, baffling scientists. Scientists finally figured out why Bennu looked deceivingly different from Earth-based observations.

title: Hail Yeah! NASA Researchers Use Volunteer Observations for Hail Estimates
summary: NASA researchers use volunteer observations to estimate hailstone sizes and predict the impact of hail on structures.

title: A Bit of Gray on an Emerald Isle
summary: Ireland's Burren region stands out with gray colors in satellite images, despite being known for its green landscapes.

summary: Another busy launch manifest is scheduled for this week, with nine launches expected from various organizations.

summary: SpaceX accelerates its ambitious goal of sending humans to the Moon and Mars.

summary: The European Space Agency's mission to discover Earth-like exoplanets, Plato, has been sealed in the Large Space Simulator chamber for vital tests under space-like conditions.

summary: Demand in space industry is skyrocketing but supply chain isn't keeping up. Policymakers can speed things up, according to two leading industry groups.

summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to build up its sovereign launch capabilities.

summary: Leonardo's missile-defense demo in Ukraine aims to become a blueprint for other nations, positioning itself as a mass distributor of space-enabled missile defense shields.

title: Telesat’s Lightspeed Service Launch Slips to 2028

title: SSC Space Inks New Launch Deal
